questions.q: Paragraph: The exponent of prime number p in n ! is given by $\left[\frac{\mathrm{n}}{\mathrm{p}}\right]+\left[\frac{\mathrm{n}}{\mathrm{p}^2}\right]+\ldots .$. or $\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\infty}\left[\frac{\mathrm{n}}{\mathrm{p}^{\mathrm{r}}}\right]$ where $[x]$ is greatest integer less than or equal to $x$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: The exponent of prime number 7 in $300!$ is -
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the number $\mathrm{N}=10800$.Question: Number of ordered triplets $(x, y, z)$ of positive integers such that $x y z=N$, is
questions.solution:
(i) Clearly, 2 element set $\{\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}\}=\frac{\text { Number of divisors of } \mathrm{N}}{2}=\frac{5 \times 4 \times 3}{2}=30$. Ans.
(ii) $10800=2^4 \cdot 3^3 \cdot 5^2$

Consider a, b, c as beggars and powers on 2,3 and 5 as identical objects to be distributed in a, b, c.

Hence number of ways is
$\left[\begin{array}{ll}
{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_2 & \text { O O O O } \times \times \\
\times{ }^5 \mathrm{C}_2 & \text { O O O } \times \times \\
\times{ }^4 \mathrm{C}_2 & \text { O O } \times \times
\end{array}\right.$
$\Rightarrow$ Total $=15 \cdot 10 \cdot 6=900$. Ans.
NOTE: ${ }^{60} \mathrm{C}_2$ is wrong because if we take two divisiors as $2^3 \times 3^3 \times 5^2$ and $2^4 \times 3^3 \times 5^2$ then their product will exceed the number 10800.
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{(iii)} \text { Sum }=\left(2+2^2+2^3+2^4\right)\left(3^1+3^2+3^3\right)\left(1+5^1+5^2\right) \\
& \quad=30 \times 39 \times 31=930 \times 39=36270=1170 \mathrm{k} \text { (given) }
\end{aligned}$
So, $k=31$. Ans.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the number $\mathrm{N}=10800$.Question: If the sum of all the divisors of N which are divisible by both 2 and 3 is 1170 k , then k is
questions.solution:
(i) Clearly, 2 element set $\{\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}\}=\frac{\text { Number of divisors of } \mathrm{N}}{2}=\frac{5 \times 4 \times 3}{2}=30$. Ans.
(ii) $10800=2^4 \cdot 3^3 \cdot 5^2$

Consider a, b, c as beggars and powers on 2,3 and 5 as identical objects to be distributed in a, b, c.

Hence number of ways is
$\left[\begin{array}{ll}
{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_2 & \text { O O O O } \times \times \\
\times{ }^5 \mathrm{C}_2 & \text { O O O } \times \times \\
\times{ }^4 \mathrm{C}_2 & \text { O O } \times \times
\end{array}\right.$
$\Rightarrow$ Total $=15 \cdot 10 \cdot 6=900$. Ans.
NOTE: ${ }^{60} \mathrm{C}_2$ is wrong because if we take two divisiors as $2^3 \times 3^3 \times 5^2$ and $2^4 \times 3^3 \times 5^2$ then their product will exceed the number 10800.
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{(iii)} \text { Sum }=\left(2+2^2+2^3+2^4\right)\left(3^1+3^2+3^3\right)\left(1+5^1+5^2\right) \\
& \quad=30 \times 39 \times 31=930 \times 39=36270=1170 \mathrm{k} \text { (given) }
\end{aligned}$
So, $k=31$. Ans.
